title: A phase that LEGITIMATELY produces a non-conforming deliverable (intent delta's "[intent-unchanged]" → SKIPPED) now pays the full ~3s settle window every invocation, because verifyReconcileDeliverable waits reconcileSettleRetries×interval for a file that will never pass full Verify. Short-circuit the wait for declared non-conforming outputs.
root_cause: After the file-authoritative verdict-source fix (fix/verdict-source-file-authoritative), a CONTRACTED phase reads the on-disk deliverable and, on !res.OK, waits the full settle window (reconcileSettleRetries=15 × reconcileSettleInterval=200ms ≈ 3s, go/internal/phases/runner/runner.go) before concluding. That window exists to catch a LATE clean-exit flush of a deliverable that WILL become well-formed. But some phases legitimately emit a deliverable that NEVER passes full Verify yet is a valid non-ship outcome — the canonical case is intent delta mode: an "[intent-unchanged]" body classifies SKIPPED but is not a full intent contract, so Verify never returns OK and the phase burns the whole ~3s window on every delta-skip. Pre-existing in spirit (the settle-retry always ran for !res.OK) but the wait was ~600ms before the cycle-921 widening to ~3s; the file-authoritative fix keeps 3s.
fix: Short-circuit the settle-WAIT for legitimately non-conforming deliverables. Options: (A) let a phase declare (via its contract or hook) that a specific marker is a terminal non-ship output, so the runner skips the wait once that marker is present; (B) have the intent phase detect delta-skip BEFORE bridge dispatch and return SKIPPED without producing a contract deliverable at all (cleanest — no wait, no Verify); (C) make the settle-wait condition-based on FILE STABILITY (size unchanged across two stats) rather than Verify-OK, so a stable-but-non-conforming file exits the wait immediately. Prefer (B) for intent specifically; (A)/(C) generalize. Do NOT reduce reconcileSettleRetries — that is the liveness ceiling for genuine late flushes.
acceptance: A delta-skip (or other declared non-conforming) phase returns its verdict WITHOUT waiting the full reconcileSettleRetries window; a regression test asserts the verify probe count / elapsed is bounded well below the full window for that case.
Genuine late-flush behavior is unchanged: a valid deliverable that flushes late still settles within the window and is classified from the file.
The ship-guard is untouched: a non-conforming deliverable still cannot launder a ship-eligible verdict.
go test -race on internal/phases/runner + internal/phases/intent green; go-review clears.
source: go-review of fix/verdict-source-file-authoritative (the architectural verdict-source fix superseding the settle-window band-aid), finding on intent-delta latency.
